# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] bd close <id1> <id2> ...   (close completed issues)
```



## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: stealth mode (no git ops)
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

## Persistent Memories (27)

Stored via `bd remember`. Update in place with `bd remember --key <key> "new content"`. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`. Remove with `bd forget <key>`.

### autosession-bead-mutation-schema-auto-session-coordinator-us
autosession-bead-mutation-schema: Auto-session coordinator uses metadata for cardinality-1 classifications (agent=human|claude|shared set on first claim, autosession_slug=<slug> rewritten each claim — last-toucher semantics, queryable via bd list --metadata-field) and labels only for transient operational state (autosession-deferred, autosession-blocked — added on defer/block, removed on next claim). No accumulating src:autosession-<slug> labels — the full attribution history lives in notes with the required prefix '[autosession/<slug> <ISO-8601>]', filterable via bd list --notes-contains. Rationale: metadata enforces cardinality invariants; labels accumulate and become noise across multi-session re-touches; notes carry transition rationale inline. Aligned 2026-04-21.

### autosession-memory-format-bd-remember-entries-added-by
autosession-memory-format: bd remember entries added by the autosession coordinator must start with 'autosession[<slug>]:' and end with a pointer to the session's exit-summary.md for audit provenance. Example format: 'autosession[tooling-wave-1-3-20260421]: <fact> (see tasks/sessions/tooling-wave-1-3-20260421/exit-summary.md#discoveries)'. The prefix lets 'bd memories autosession' surface the cohort and 'bd memories autosession[<slug>]' narrow to one session. The pointer lets /import-session reviewers drill back to context without guessing. Gated by lifecycle.memory.can_remember in the beads template.

### autosession-preship-bead-topology
Topology lesson from 2026-04-22 auto-session pre-ship bead creation. (1) bd mol/bond is for reusable WORKFLOW TEMPLATES (protos/molecules), NOT for general-purpose epic grouping or taxonomic nesting — use parent-child for hierarchical containment, bond only when composing template workflows. (2) bd enforces a directional constraint: tasks cannot block epics ('epics can only block other epics, not tasks'). Workaround: create a dedicated release-gate task as a child of the epic; route hard 'blocks' edges from external tasks through the release-gate task. Epic-completion semantics then handle the transitive gate. (3) 'discovered-from' edges should trace the actual causal chain — the investigation where a fact was uncovered — not the consumer bead that acts on the fact. (4) For aggregates of one-off work with independent children, a plain epic beats molecule primitives; bd mol distill can promote an ad-hoc epic into a proto LATER if a reusable pattern emerges. (5) Closed-at-creation provenance beads (filing completed research as a task for downstream discovered-from anchors) are a valid and useful pattern — the bead's purpose is audit-trail, not workflow.

### autosession-status-transition-mechanics-bd-1-0-2
autosession-status-transition-mechanics: bd 1.0.2 has no per-transition metadata for status changes other than close (bd close --reason). Non-close transitions use 'bd update --status <new> --append-notes "[autosession/<slug> <ISO-8601>] <from> -> <new>; <rationale>"' in one invocation — notes accumulate in the single notes field, are timestamped by the coordinator-provided prefix, and are filterable via --notes-contains. Respect user's custom statuses (bd config set status.custom review,testing): coordinator default on-finish = review, not closed.

### bd-formula-not-for-runtime-policy-bd-formulas
bd-formula-not-for-runtime-policy: bd formulas describe issue-DAG spawn templates (variables, steps, parent/child edges) cooked into protos and poured into real beads. They do NOT constrain runtime coordinator behavior on existing beads — allowed status transitions, note prefixes, label can/can't-touch, metadata rewrite timing all live outside the formula layer. When a policy is ~80% runtime behavior and ~20% create-time, ship YAML-read-by-coordinator and skip the formula conversion; partial conversion splits the policy across two mental models without payoff. Re-evaluate if bd grows runtime-policy primitives (e.g., a bd gate shape blocking out-of-allowlist transitions). Verified 2026-04-22 via J121-7bv.1 probe: bd formula list ships zero bundled examples, schema is documented only via --help, formulas failed validation on both flat and wrapped JSON attempts.

### bd-jira-sync-pull-incremental-timing-gap-sync
bd jira sync --pull incremental timing gap: sync appends AND updated >= <last-sync-timestamp> to JQL, so tickets not updated since the last sync run are silently skipped even if they match the base JQL filter. Root cause of BOCO-18077 missing on initial session sync (2026-04-21). Fix: run 'bd jira sync --pull' again after any period of inactivity to catch stale-but-active tickets; there is no --full-rescan flag. Coverage check: compare 'bd list --label=src:jira | wc -l' against Jira API total. Also: 3 tickets (BOCO-16303, BOCO-17538, BOCO-18128) permanently absent because they were Done before the first full sync ran — not worth importing.

### bd-list-json-trailing-footer
bd list --json appends a human-readable 'Showing N issues...' footer AFTER the JSON array when results are truncated by --limit (default 50). This breaks strict JSON parsers. Workaround: pipe through `sed -n '1,/^]$/p'` to cut at the closing bracket line. bd ready --json does NOT have this bug. Affects: /show slash command (J121-9kp.2.15), any future tooling that consumes bd list --json output.

### beads-reconcile-design-j121-1b1-design-doc-for
beads-reconcile-design-J121-1b1: Design doc for label-inheritance architecture lives in J121-1b1's description + design fields (bd show J121-1b1). Hybrid C+D+E: (C) bd-timew wrapper forward-propagates inheritable labels to new children, (D) beads-reconcile script walks parent-child edges and materializes missing inheritable dimensions, (E) epics declare the canonical tuple once for org transparency. E alone doesn't work — bd --label is exact-match, doesn't walk graph. See also: billing-labels-on-jira-beads-all-src-jira for the 2026-04-21 manual pass that established the pattern. Resume the design conversation by reading bd show J121-1b1's design field — 7 open questions are captured there.

### beads-viewer-license-risk-accepted
Decision 2026-04-20 (mhaynes): bv (beads_viewer, github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer) upstream LICENSE is MIT-with-rider that explicitly denies rights to OpenAI, Anthropic, their Affiliates, and 'any person or entity acting directly or indirectly on behalf of' them — including 'copying, modifying, merging, ..., executing, benchmarking, testing, analyzing, indexing, or incorporating the Software into any ... evaluation harness, or pipeline for machine learning or other automated systems.' Risk NOT cleared for: (a) team-wide or BOCO-facing adoption; (b) incorporation into billable deliverables; (c) public redistribution of derivative tooling. Integration surfaces that invoke --robot-*: /next (delegated to --robot-next via J121 bead-for-refactor), /triage (planned, new bead J121-tbd), /session-checkpoint enrichment (planned). ### claude-settings-location-editormode
Decision 2026-04-20 (confirmed): editorMode=vim must live in ~/.claude.json, NOT ~/.claude/settings.json. User tested settings.json placement and Claude Code ignored it. Fix deployed via chezmoi 'modify' script at ~/.local/share/chezmoi/home/modify_private_dot_claude.json — jq pipeline that surgically sets .editorMode without chezmoi owning the full file. Prefix order matters: modify_private_ (not private_modify_) — with private_ first, chezmoi consumes only the private_ attribute and treats 'modify_dot_claude.json' as a literal filename. Script is defensive: passes stdin through unchanged if jq missing; exits 0 on empty stdin (target doesn't exist) to avoid stubbing a file Claude Code might overwrite without merging. Executable bit not needed on source (chezmoi copies contents to temp file and sets bit). private_ attribute preserves the 0600 mode on ~/.claude.json (holds OAuth tokens). Claude Code picks up editorMode changes live — no restart required (confirmed 2026-04-20). Rejected full-file chezmoi-management: 47KB of 64KB churn-heavy state (projects, counters, timestamps) that would conflict across machines and leak oauthAccount in a git remote.

Estimate label convention: three dimensions — est-h:N (hours, only dimension bd-timew variance reports consult), est-p:N (story points, scrum), est-t:S (t-shirt size: XS/S/M/L/XL, scrum). ### rano-adoption-counter-rationale
Decision 2026-04-21 (mhaynes): install rano (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/rano) as BOCO AI governance / telemetry PoC, OVERRIDING the dicklesworthstone-review agent's 'skip' verdict. Counter-rationale: (a) negligible processing cost (/proc polling, sub-ms overhead), (b) manageable storage (SQLite, rotate-able), (c) zero token cost (out-of-process, no MCP injection), (d) BOCO is actively seeking AI governance tooling — Erick already standardized New Relic telemetry for devs (see ~/.zshrc env vars) — so pre-installing rano positions it as a potential reporting/auditing data source for BOCO AI policy. Current scope: personal PoC, not team-wide; success metric = 'useful data accumulates' rather than 'immediate problem solved'. License rider applies (same OpenAI/Anthropic Restricted Parties clause as bv); risk accepted for personal-tooling scope under same reasoning as bd memories beads-viewer-license-risk-accepted. If PoC yields actionable patterns, next step is proposing to Erick as part of BOCO's AI governance posture. Implementation: Chezmoi installer, systemd user unit for daemon, weekly email report via systemd timer. Full review: tasks/dicklesworthstone-review/deliverables/dicklesworthstone_projects.md (rano section).

### show-vs-next-split
Decision 2026-04-20: /show and /next are separate slash commands, not one command with modes. /show = read-only filter+list over bd list/bd ready with ergonomic flag names (--task/--epic/--area/--service/--assignee/--status); /next = scoring/recommendation layered on top of bd ready. Rationale: single responsibility, /show answers 'what exists that matches X?', /next answers 'what should I work on next?' — mixing them forces every /show invocation to load scoring heuristics it doesn't need. Beads: J121-9kp.2.15 (/show), J121-9kp.2.13 (/next). Alternative considered: merge into /next with --filter mode flag — rejected as premature abstraction.

### slash-command-frontmatter-conventions-apply-whenever-editing
Slash command frontmatter conventions (apply whenever editing any ~/.claude/commands/*.md or project .claude/commands/*.md file): (1) argument-hint field must accurately reflect all accepted flags after every edit — update it whenever flags are added, removed, or renamed; (2) timestamps array is append-only: add a new entry {action: updated, at: <ISO-8601>, actor: <git user.name>, note: <one-line summary of change>} for every edit; (3) comments array: add a new bullet for substantive changes (new flag families, behavioral shifts); mechanical fixes go in timestamps[].note only — do not duplicate in comments. These conventions are enforced by /edit-command step 7 invariants.

### smartstore-themes-working-branch-is-origin-prototype-not
SmartStore_Themes working branch is origin/Prototype not origin/master. Local master tracks Prototype (0 ahead/behind). origin/master is stale/unused — ignore 103-ahead sweep warning. All theme work flows through Prototype branch.

### svc-labels-unused-no-beads-currently-have-svc
svc-labels-unused: No beads currently have svc: labels even though the label taxonomy supports them and bd-timew.yaml maps area: labels to billing tuples. User flagged this 2026-04-21 as a gap to fix. When adding svc: labels, the value should match the Svc field from bd-timew start output (e.g., svc:Technology-Services). Start by adding to all billable beads under BOCO-18077 and BOCO-17078.

### terraform-drift-diagnostic-procedure
When a terraform plan shows unexpected drift (force-replace, mysterious in-place updates), triangulate from three sources: (1) git log -G '<attribute>' -- <tf-file> to find when the attribute was added + git blame for the author/rationale; (2) Snowflake SNOWFLAKE.ACCOUNT_USAGE.QUERY_HISTORY with query_text ilike filter on the affected DDL (365-day retention, shows terraform_provider_usage_tracking comments identifying TF-driven vs manual); (3) Azure blob last-modified on the tfstate file (az storage blob list --account-name journeyblobstorage --container-name tfstate) cross-referenced against the fix commit's timestamp. Hypothesis: attribute added to code, applied to some envs but not others. Example: J121-n39.4 2026-04-22, DEV found stuck pre-commit-0ea822d while TEST+PROD were applied post-commit. Blob versioning is NOT enabled on journeyblobstorage as of 2026-04-22; only last-modified is available — no per-apply history until J121-h1x is resolved.

### terraform-proc-owner-grants-truncate-combined-with-dml
terraform-proc-owner-grants-truncate-combined-with-dml: In terraform/modules/snowflake/grants.tf, TRUNCATE is combined with DML (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) in the proc_owner_mdm_tables and proc_owner_mdm_future_tables resources rather than as a separate grant block. Rationale: every proc that truncates an MDM table also does DML on it, so splitting would inflate the state graph without clarifying intent. Diverges from the original J121-n39.1 plan which specified three blocks (create_table + truncate + dml). If that invariant ever breaks (a proc truncates a table it has no DML on), split TRUNCATE back into its own resource.

### time-tracking-option-a-decision
Decision 2026-04-18: time-tracking billing-tuple storage = Option A (sidecar config .beads/bd-timew.yaml with pattern-based label mappings), NOT Option B (merged |-delimited labels on every issue). Why: per-issue tuple duplication (~95% of issues share the same tuple); NetSuite case strings contain spaces/punctuation that break Beads dimension:value label grammar and are Jira-sync-unsafe; case renames would force bulk relabeling; merged labels defeat the Option B prefix-namespaced label taxonomy. Wrapper: bd start <id> reads issue labels, resolves against sidecar, runs timew start with client:/case:/svc: tags. Convention details in auto-memory feedback-time-tracking.md. J121 sidecar at .beads/bd-timew.yaml (patterns list currently a scaffold - full NetSuite case list still needed).

### title-convention-for-src-jira-beads-always-prepend
Title convention for src:jira beads: always prepend the Jira key as 'BOCO-XXXXX · <original title>'. Example: 'BOCO-18077 · Great Clips | Pipeline Review/Validation'. Apply when creating via bd create or when a jira sync imports a new ticket. Rationale: bd list and bd show surface the Jira key immediately without needing to inspect external_ref. Applied retroactively to all src:jira beads on 2026-04-21.

### tutor-command-scope
Decision 2026-04-20: /tutor slash command is scoped to the shareable Claude config repo initiative (J121-014), NOT J121-9kp Wave 2. Rationale: in isolation /tutor overlaps with the planned /docs command (J121-9kp.2.4); bundled with the shareable repo it earns its keep as discoverability for users who didn't build the tools themselves. The --provenance flag forces a useful discipline: per-tool source:/decided: frontmatter that skills and commands should probably carry anyway. Expected flags: --short/--long, --provenance, --history, --example[s].

Let's continue with the autosession work.  I have a few feature ideas:
- I'd like to add an "interactivity lever" to the command that allows the user to specify the desired degree of interactivity for the autosession.  At the extreme end of the scale, the user should be able to specify a --headless flag to have the generated documents, tool-use permissions, etc. scoped to support a fully-autonomous coordination agent; the generated autosession init script would spawn a headless Claude Code instance in this case.
- I'd also like to expand the types of notifications offered by the hooks and supporting scripts we've added.  In particular: I had a different Claude instance help me setup phone notifications via ntfy.sh this morning, and the resulting notifications acted as a useful "remote tether" to the work I had Claude doing while I stepped away from my desk for awhile.  This was useful enough that I'd like to add hooks and supporting scripts to support adding ntfy.sh-based alerts to the autosession command.
  - **Open question:** Are ntfy.sh topics bidirectional?  In other words, can I *reply* to a notification my phone receives from the ntfy app by sending a "response notification" on the same named topic?  If so, this would allow remote Claude communication and activity--a feature I'd definitely like to enable if possible.
  - **Architectural issue:** Ideally, hooks should be *composable*, i.e., the user should be able to specify multiple notification types and have the command generate the appropriate backend hook configuration to support the specified composition.  Does Claude Code's hook configuration schema allow multiple hooks to be registered for the same event?
    - Following this line of thought: The user should be able to specify any executable, not just those in the command's scripts/ directory--those are just there for reusability, convenience, and a form of documentation-as-code (i.e., "this is what a hook script should look like").
    - For improved ergonomics, we could include a "hooks" YAML template similar to what we built for beads ceremony rules, allowing the user to configure notifiers, compaction responders (e.g., "context rehydraters", especially for fully-autonomous agents), etc., in addition to a generic hook config fallback.
      - If we go this route, I think there's an increasingly good argument for consolidating all our YAML config templates--for beads ceremony, permissions, notifications/hooks, agent interactivity level, repo preferences, etc.--into a single config template that the user can either (a) install globally, with default preferences, or (b) override on a per-autosession basis on scaffolding.  Because it's an LLM tool, we could even allow users to mix natural-language and YAML-based configs to a certain extent--for example, by providing a "hook_prompt" or "repo_prompt"
    - A possible safety mechanism would be to only allow autosessions to run hook command executables in an autogenerated scripts/ subdirectory of the session's working dir, but to have the scaffolding script create symlinks or Bash shims pointing to any user-specified scripts or executables the user specifies as hooks.  Taking this to an extreme, particularly for fully-autonomous autosessions, the user could specify a "command whitelist" and have the scaffolder create shims for every command on the whitelist, place them in the scripts/ working dir, and then simply grant the agent blanket permissions of the form "allow commands like $(pwd)/scripts/*".  Does this buy us anything, either in terms of safety or architecturally?

We'd still need to generate wrappers or shims if we want to continue using the hook/notification logging system we've built (side note: now that we're using project-localized hook configs, is this even necessary?), but we've already built the tooling for this.
    - For improved ergonomics, we could include a "hooks" YAML template similar to what we built for beads ceremony rules, allowing the user to configure notifiers, compaction responders (e.g., "context rehydraters", especially for fully-autonomous agents), etc., in addition to a generic hook config fallback.

Because it's an LLM tool, we could even allow users to mix natural-language and YAML-based configs to a certain extent--for example, by providing a "hook_prompt" or "repo_prompt" YAML config key that supplements the structured config by pointing to a user-provided Markdown file specifying the desired behavior.

For instance, could we achieve a similar sandboxing effectg by using `git worktree add` to create a session-owned worktree? We've built a hook wrapper / command watchdog system that allows us to inject logging and anti-hang features into arbitrary executables. This could allow us to bypass user-interaction events with a standard "no-op or fail" response in case the user wants full agent autonomy, for example.
  - Would allowing the user to specify their desired logging command or executable and then injecting it into the wrapper script make sense?
    - For instance, the user could specify a logging script that takes structured input shaped like a Claude hook invocation, flattens it into a database record, and inserts it into a target SQLite database within the autosession dir.
  - Another semi-related idea: would an autosession-scoped "beads sandbox" make sense?  The coordinator would have "admin"-level rights to the autosession's beads instance, and read access to a project beads instance if the user requests it, and could import beads, formulas, molecules, etc. from the project beads instance into the autosession-scoped beads instance for planning, agent coordination, and execution.

There's a lot to cover here--take your time and review each idea and question thoroughly, then give me your thoughts.  Request clarification on any part of this prompt if you need it.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: auto-session
3	description: Scaffold and launch a semi-autonomous multi-bead implementation session. TRIGGER on `/auto-session`, "start an autonomous session on <epic>", "kick off autonomous work on <bead-group>", or "resume the paused session". The skill elicits scope, per-target-repo sandbox (location × branch, each pinnable by repo template), model tiers, landmark cadence, alerts policy, and bead mutation policy; scaffolds a session dir with a session-scoped `.claude/settings.local.json`, a compaction-resilient `CLAUDE.md`, a launcher `run.sh`, a human execution log, JSONL event stream, categorized scratchpad, and decision log; gates on explicit user approval before the user launches the coordinator. Loads opt-in per-repo templates (e.g., chezmoi), convention templates, alerts templates, and beads templates. Does NOT fire on single-bead work (`/start` + `/stop` covers that) or execution of a fixed phased plan (use `claude-mem:do`).
4	author: Michael Haynes
5	scope: global
6	tags: [autonomous, session, multi-bead, coordinator, review, decision-log, high-blast-radius]
7	timestamps:
8	  - action: created
9	    at: 2026-04-21T17:57:47-05:00
10	    actor: Michael Haynes
11	  - action: revised
12	    at: 2026-04-22T00:35:00-05:00
13	    actor: Michael Haynes
14	    note: "Session-scoped hooks replace global marker-file gating; CLAUDE.md + SessionStart:compact hook added for compaction resilience; templates/alerts/ and templates/beads/ introduced; install.sh reduced to agent-only symlink; scaffold-autosession.sh added."
15	comments:
16	  - "Source: design conversation 2026-04-21 on setting up a secondary autonomous session alongside a primary interactive session for the tooling-and-claude-related bead epics (Waves 1-3). Iterative design dialogue covered sandbox isolation, event logging, decision protocols, subagent patterns, notification ergonomics, bd metadata/label/notes taxonomy, and compaction resilience."
17	  - "Motivation: prevents the main failure modes of autonomous sessions — silent clobbering of the canonical chezmoi main branch, decision drift without audit trail, subagent context starvation, timew interval orphaning, missed notifications when the user steps away, bead-label proliferation, and compaction-induced policy paraphrasing. Encodes the structural decisions so every autonomous session starts from the same tested baseline."
18	  - "Projected use: fires when the user kicks off or resumes a multi-bead autonomous working session. Should NOT fire on single-bead work, on execution of a pre-existing phased plan (claude-mem:do handles that), or on interactive one-off implementation. Bundled with the session-reviewer agent (symlinked into ~/.claude/agents/ via install.sh, or per-session via scaffold-autosession.sh)."
19	---
20	
21	# Auto Session
22	
23	Scaffold and launch a semi-autonomous multi-bead implementation session. Everything structural — working dir layout, sandbox discipline, logging, decision protocol, subagent patterns, notifications, bead mutation policy, compaction resilience — is codified here so each session starts from the same tested baseline.
24	
25	The skill is **repo-agnostic at its core**. Repo-specific conventions (chezmoi sandbox rules, push policies, render-command allowlists) live in opt-in templates under `templates/repos/`, loaded per session.
26	
27	## When to fire
28	
29	- `/auto-session` slash-invocation
30	- "Start an autonomous session on `<scope>`"
31	- "Kick off autonomous work on `<epic>` / `<bead-group>`"
32	- "Resume the paused session" / "/auto-session --resume"
33	
34	## When NOT to fire
35	
36	- Single-bead work — `/start <id>` + `/stop` is the right tool
37	- Execution of a pre-planned phased roadmap — use `claude-mem:do`
38	- Interactive one-off implementation — just do the work directly
39	
40	## Architectural pillars
41	
42	Four design decisions shape everything else; read them first.
43	
44	1. **Session-scoped hooks.** Each session writes its own `<session-dir>/.claude/settings.local.json` with the Notification / PreToolUse / SessionStart:compact hooks. Claude Code only loads that file when launched with cwd == session dir, so interactive sessions are unaffected. No global marker file, no dispatcher-side gating — the hook's *presence in the session's config* is the signal.
45	2. **Compaction-resilient CLAUDE.md.** Each session's `CLAUDE.md` carries the rehydrate rule, anti-loop clause, and single-writer invariants. Because Claude Code auto-loads the cwd's `CLAUDE.md` at every session start (including post-compact), these rules survive when `prompt.md` is paraphrased away. A SessionStart:compact hook belts-and-suspenders it by emitting a rehydrate instruction into the coordinator's context.
46	3. **Bd metadata + structured notes, not label proliferation.** The coordinator sets cardinality-1 metadata (`agent=claude`, `autosession_slug=<slug>`) on claim, and writes prefixed `bd note` entries on every status transition. Transient state uses a tiny label set (`autosession-deferred`, `autosession-blocked`), each removed on the next claim. No accumulating `src:autosession-<slug>` history labels — the notes trail is the audit history.
47	4. **Launcher-enforced cwd invariant.** `run.sh` refuses to launch unless its own location matches the expected session dir. This prevents the silent-misbehavior class of failure where hooks, CLAUDE.md, and bead paths all resolve against the wrong project because the user typed `claude` from the parent directory.
48	
49	## First-run install (global — one-time)
50	
51	Autonomous sessions are self-contained: each session dir carries its own hooks, CLAUDE.md, and settings file. The only *global* step is symlinking the `session-reviewer` subagent so it resolves from `Task(subagent_type=session-reviewer)` in any Claude Code session.
52	
53	```sh
54	~/.claude/skills/auto-session/install.sh
55	```
56	
57	`install.sh` is **TTY-only** — it refuses to run from a non-interactive shell. Modes: `install` (default), `uninstall`, `status`. Idempotent; re-running detects existing wiring and either confirms or surfaces drift.
58	
59	If the user is chezmoi-managed and declares the symlink in `home/dot_claude/agents/symlink_session-reviewer.md`, `chezmoi apply` handles it and `install.sh` detects the pre-existing symlink as already-installed.
60	
61	The scaffold script also symlinks the agent into each session's local `.claude/agents/`, so `install.sh` is not strictly required — it just makes `session-reviewer` usable outside auto-sessions.
62	
63	## Session lifecycle
64	
65	```
66	┌────────────────────┐
67	│ 1. Scope elicit    │  goals, beads/epics, target repos, model tiers, landmarks
68	├────────────────────┤
69	│ 2. Sandbox elicit  │  per-repo (location, branch) + read_only pre-empt
70	├────────────────────┤
71	│ 3. Template load   │  repos/, conventions/, permissions/, alerts/, beads/
72	├────────────────────┤
73	│ 4. Scaffold        │  scaffold-autosession.sh materializes dir tree, CLAUDE.md,
74	│                    │  run.sh, .claude/settings.local.json, state/
75	├────────────────────┤
76	│ 5. Approval gate   │  present prompt.md + scaffold tree + resolved sandbox/policy;
77	│                    │  require explicit "go"
78	├────────────────────┤
79	│ 6. Launch          │  instruct user to run `cd <session-dir> && ./run.sh`
80	├────────────────────┤
81	│ 7. Execution loop  │  coordinator works through beads; hooks fire; compaction-safe
82	├────────────────────┤
83	│ 8. Landmarks       │  every N closures: gap-review subagent; session-reviewer on branches
84	├────────────────────┤
85	│ 9. Shutdown        │  close timew, push pre-authorized repos, write exit-summary
86	└────────────────────┘
87	```
88	
89	### Step 1 — Scope elicitation
90	
91	Interactive prompts (one at a time; don't front-load):
92	
93	1. **Session slug** — short kebab-case descriptor (e.g., `tooling-wave-1-3`). Must match `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`.
94	2. **Goal summary** — one paragraph describing what the session is trying to accomplish.
95	3. **Bead scope** — beads to include. Accept:
96	   - Explicit list: `J121-9kp J121-xxx J121-yyy`
97	   - Epic prefix: `J121-9kp.*` (skill expands via `bd list`)
98	   - Label filter: `bd list --label=area:tooling --status=open --json` (skill shows count, asks to confirm)
99	   - Native `bd query` expression for complex AND/OR/NOT filters.
100	4. **Target repos** — paths that may be modified during the session. Each gets a sandbox in step 2.
101	5. **Model tier policy** — default tiers; user can override:
102	   - `haiku` for review/gap-analysis/logging subagents
103	   - `sonnet` for implementation (default)
104	   - `opus` for architecture decisions the coordinator escalates
105	6. **Landmark cadence** — number of bead closures between gap-review subagent invocations (default: 5).
106	7. **Max session duration** — wall-clock cap (default: 6 hours); coordinator halts for user confirmation past this.
107	
108	### Step 2 — Sandbox elicitation
109	
110	For each target repo from step 1.4, ask two orthogonal questions (or accept `read_only: true` which skips both):
111	
112	**Q1: Location** — where does the working copy live?
113	- `in-place` (default) — operate on the repo in its canonical filesystem location
114	- `clone` — clone to `<session-dir>/clones/<repo-name>/`; canonical stays untouched; push feature branches to canonical at shutdown
115	
116	**Q2: Branch** — which branch do commits land on?
117	- `current` (default) — commit to the repo's currently checked-out branch
118	- `feature` — create `feature/auto-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>` from HEAD; commit there; never merge during session
119	
120	All four `(location, branch)` combinations are valid:
121	
122	| Location | Branch | Typical use |
123	|---|---|---|
124	| `in-place` | `current` | `.beads/`, `tasks/` — repos where main absorbs session commits |
125	| `in-place` | `feature` | Changes need review before landing, but in-place visibility is wanted |
126	| `clone` | `feature` | Main must stay usable by concurrent sessions (chezmoi) |
127	| `clone` | `current` | Rare — sandbox testing against main without affecting canonical users |
128	
129	If a per-repo template under `templates/repos/` matches (by `repo_paths` or `repo_name_patterns`), it may pin one or both dimensions. The skill surfaces this as *"template X pins `location=clone`, `branch=feature`; accept?"*. Pinned dimensions override user answers for safety; the user may decline the repo entirely but cannot downgrade a pin.
130	
131	### Step 3 — Template loading
132	
133	Five template categories:
134	
135	- **Repo templates** (`templates/repos/*.md`) — auto-loaded when a target repo matches `applies_to` in the template frontmatter.
136	- **Convention templates** (`templates/conventions/*.md`) — opt-in; skill lists them with descriptions and asks which to include.
137	- **Permissions templates** (`templates/permissions/*.yaml`) — opt-in; skill loads `default.yaml` (or user-specified), shows the commented groups, asks which to uncomment, then merges selected entries into the **parent project's** `.claude/settings.local.json`.
138	- **Alerts templates** (`templates/alerts/*.yaml`) — selects the notification-category policy (sounds, urgency, nag cadence). `default.yaml` is used unless the user picks a variant. Embedded into `prompt.md` so the coordinator knows when to fire each category.
139	- **Beads templates** (`templates/beads/*.yaml`) — declares the `lifecycle` policy the coordinator follows for every bead it touches: status transitions, metadata set on claim, required transition notes, allowed/forbidden labels, whether `bd create` and `bd remember` are permitted. `default.yaml` is used unless the user picks a variant. Embedded into `prompt.md` and surfaced to the review agent.
140	
141	Repo, convention, alerts, and beads templates are embedded verbatim into `prompt.md`. Permissions templates are parsed and merged into `.claude/settings.local.json`, not embedded. The skill does not interpret the body text of repo/convention templates — it just loads and embeds.
142	
143	### Step 4 — Scaffold
144	
145	The skill invokes `scripts/scaffold-autosession.sh --session-dir <path> --slug <slug>`, which materializes:
146	
147	```
148	<project-root>/tasks/sessions/<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>/
149	  prompt.md                        ← verbatim session prompt (audit artifact; coordinator reads this)
150	  CLAUDE.md                        ← compaction-resilient rules (rendered from templates/session-claude-md.md)
151	  run.sh                           ← launcher (rendered from templates/run.sh.tmpl; chmod +x)
152	  state.json                       ← resumable checkpoint
153	  execution-log.md                 ← human-readable narrative (coordinator appends)
154	  events.jsonl                     ← machine-readable event stream (all agents append; POSIX-atomic)
155	  decisions.md                     ← decision log with reversion plans
156	  exit-summary.md                  ← (written at shutdown from templates/exit-summary.md)
157	  hook.log                         ← JSONL two-record hook audit (written by scripts/lib/hook-wrapper.sh)
158	  hook.log.lock                    ← flock target for serialized writes
159	  .claude/
160	    settings.local.json            ← session-scoped hooks (Notification, PreToolUse, SessionStart:compact)
161	    agents/
162	      session-reviewer.md          ← symlink into the skill's agents/ dir
163	  state/
164	    README.md
165	    nag-<session-id>.pid           ← (transient; created by hook dispatcher while io-block is active)
166	  clones/                          ← clone-sandbox repos land here (one subdir per repo)
167	  agents/                          ← per-subagent working dirs (brief.md, result.md, log.md each)
168	  execution-log/                   ← per-turn narrative snippets, if the coordinator splits the log
169	  scratchpad/
170	    assertions/                    ← facts the coordinator has verified
171	    conventions/                   ← loaded convention templates + derived rules
172	    findings/                      ← per-bead discoveries
173	    goals/                         ← session goals + per-bead sub-goals
174	    hypotheses/                    ← unverified ideas the coordinator is testing
175	```
176	
177	After the scaffold returns, the skill writes `prompt.md` and any permissions merges. `prompt.md` is generated from the elicited scope, sandbox table, loaded templates (repos, conventions, alerts, beads), permissions granted, and the decision/logging/shutdown protocols. The scaffold script is idempotent but refuses to overwrite a non-empty dir without `--force`.
178	
179	### Step 5 — Approval gate
180	
181	Skill displays:
182	- Full `prompt.md` contents
183	- Scaffold tree (abbreviated; depth 3)
184	- Sandbox table (repo → location → branch → rationale; pinned dimensions marked)
185	- Resolved model tiers
186	- Landmark cadence
187	- Bead mutation policy summary (from the active `templates/beads/*.yaml`)
188	- Alerts policy summary (from the active `templates/alerts/*.yaml`)
189	- Permissions granted
190	
191	Prompts: `Launch session? (yes / edit prompt / cancel)`. "edit prompt" opens `prompt.md` for user review; the gate re-runs after.
192	
193	### Step 6 — Launch
194	
195	On explicit "yes" the skill emits a single instruction:
196	
197	> Open a fresh terminal and run:
198	>
199	> ```sh
200	> cd <session-dir>
201	> ./run.sh
202	> ```
203	
204	**The skill does not spawn the coordinator itself.** The coordinator runs in a fresh Claude Code session the user starts manually. This keeps session context clean and gives the user a clear resume-or-abandon choice.
205	
206	`run.sh` performs three guards before `exec claude`:
207	1. Self-location check (`$SESSION_DIR_ACTUAL == $SESSION_DIR_EXPECTED`) — refuses if the session dir has moved.
208	2. `prompt.md` exists — refuses if the scaffold is corrupt.
209	3. `.claude/settings.local.json` exists — refuses, because without it hooks do not fire.
210	
211	It then exports `AUTOSESSION_SLUG` and `AUTOSESSION_DIR` (so hook scripts can attribute log records without parsing cwd), and `exec`s `claude`.
212	
213	### Step 7 — Execution loop (coordinator's responsibility)
214	
215	```
216	while beads remain and not paused and not over time cap:
217	    bead = bd ready --top 1
218	    /start <bead>                              # claim + start timew
219	    # Claim bookkeeping, per templates/beads/*.yaml lifecycle.on_claim:
220	    bd update <bead> --set-metadata agent=claude \
221	                     --set-metadata autosession_slug=<slug> \
222	                     --remove-label autosession-deferred \
223	                     --remove-label autosession-blocked
224	    bd note <bead> "[autosession/<slug> <ISO>] open -> in_progress; claimed via /auto-session"
225	
226	    implement(bead)                            # possibly via subagents
227	    verify(bead)                               # tests, dry-run renders, etc.
228	    commit(bead)                               # per-repo per-sandbox-level commit discipline
229	    log_event("bead_closed", bead)             # append JSONL + execution-log
230	
231	    # Finish bookkeeping, per lifecycle.on_finish (custom status 'review', not 'closed'):
232	    bd update <bead> --status review
233	    bd note <bead> "[autosession/<slug> <ISO>] in_progress -> review; impl complete, awaits user verification"
234	
235	    /stop                                      # release timew
236	    push pre-authorized repos                  # .beads, tasks — per project policy
237	    if closures_since_review >= landmark_cadence:
238	        gap_review_subagent()                  # Haiku; output to scratchpad/findings/
239	        closures_since_review = 0
240	    save_state()                               # overwrite state.json
241	```
242	
243	Defer / block go through lifecycle.on_defer and lifecycle.on_block respectively (see active beads template). Every status transition pairs with a `bd note` using the prefix `[autosession/<slug> <ISO>]`.
244	
245	### Step 8 — Landmark reviews
246	
247	Every `landmark_cadence` bead closures, the coordinator spawns a **gap-review subagent** (Haiku, foreground):
248	- Brief includes: recent commits, closed beads, current scratchpad contents.
249	- Output written to `agents/gap-review-<timestamp>/result.md`.
250	- Coordinator reads the summary and logs an event; full result stays in the file.
251	
252	After any commit on a `feature` or `clone` repo, the coordinator may invoke the **session-reviewer agent** (`subagent_type: "session-reviewer"`) for pattern-compliance review. Output goes to `agents/session-reviewer-<timestamp>/result.md`.
253	
254	### Step 9 — Shutdown
255	
256	Triggered by: all beads closed, time cap reached, pause signal, or user interrupt. Coordinator responsibilities:
257	
258	1. Stop any open timew interval (`bd-timew stop` if active).
259	2. For each `in-place + current` repo: commit + push if pre-authorized.
260	3. For each `feature`-branch repo (any location): push the feature branch (never merge).
261	4. For each `clone` repo: push feature branch to canonical; leave the clone in place for user review.
262	5. Update `state.json` with final state.
263	6. Append final execution-log entry.
264	7. Emit shutdown JSONL event.
265	8. Write `<session-dir>/exit-summary.md` from `templates/exit-summary.md` (do not skip — this is the input artifact for `/import-session`).
266	
267	No session-marker file to remove — hooks are session-scoped and the session's `.claude/settings.local.json` becomes inert the moment the coordinator exits.
268	
269	## Context compaction resilience
270	
271	Context compaction paraphrases the conversation, including `prompt.md` if it was pasted in. Rules embedded only in `prompt.md` can be silently paraphrased into vague guidance. Three mitigations work together:
272	
273	1. **`<session-dir>/CLAUDE.md`** (scaffolded from `templates/session-claude-md.md`). Claude Code auto-loads the cwd's CLAUDE.md at every session start and post-compact, so rules that live here survive. The scaffold puts the rehydrate rule, anti-loop clause, working-dir invariant, single-writer invariants, and bead-mutation summary here.
274	
275	2. **SessionStart:compact hook** (`scripts/lib/post-compact-rehydrate.sh`). Fires immediately after compaction; emits an `additionalContext` payload telling the coordinator to re-read `prompt.md` and CLAUDE.md before its next substantive action. Because this instruction is injected *after* compaction, it cannot be paraphrased by the compaction that just ran.
276	
277	3. **Size discipline on `prompt.md`.** Keep under ~20KB. Reference repo/convention templates by path rather than embedding them verbatim when a size threshold is exceeded. Re-reads are cheap only if the brief is small.
278	
279	**Anti-loop clause** (in `templates/session-claude-md.md`): if uncertainty about the brief persists after re-reading once, halt and fire a `decision_block` notification rather than re-reading a second time or guessing. Re-reading more than once in a compaction cycle is a sign the brief is unclear, not that the coordinator needs to try harder.
280	
281	## Bead mutation policy
282	
283	The active `templates/beads/<name>.yaml` is the authoritative policy; summary follows.
284	
285	- **Metadata (cardinality-1, last-write-wins)** — set `agent=claude` and `autosession_slug=<slug>` on every claim. Queryable via `bd list --metadata-field` and `bd list --has-metadata-key`. Use for one-shot classification the coordinator owns.
286	- **Labels (cardinality-N, user-owned taxonomy)** — the coordinator only adds transient markers: `autosession-deferred`, `autosession-blocked`. Each is removed on the next claim. Forbidden: anything under `src:`, `scope:`, or `area:` (those are classification labels owned by the user's taxonomy).
287	- **Notes (append-only audit trail)** — every status transition writes `bd note` with the prefix `[autosession/<slug> <ISO-8601>] <from> -> <to>; <rationale>`. Queryable via `bd list --notes-contains`. This is the history; there is no accumulating-label workaround.
288	- **Comments** — disabled by default. Notes are filterable, comments aren't, so notes are the right tool for everything we care about.
289	- **Description / graph** — disabled. The coordinator never appends to description and never sets parents/dependencies.
290	- **`bd create`** — disabled by default. When enabled, any created bead must carry `src:autosession-<slug>` (this is the one case where an accumulating-history label *is* warranted — to flag the provenance of machine-created beads to future humans).
291	- **`bd remember`** — enabled; prescribed format `autosession[<slug>]: <fact> (see <session-dir>/exit-summary.md#discoveries)`. Memories have `review_on_import: true` — `/import-session` surfaces them for human confirmation before they graduate to un-scoped memories.
292	- **Custom statuses respected** — `on_finish` maps to `review`, not `closed`, because the user's bd config sets `status.custom review,testing`.
293	
294	Full policy (including the `allowed_transitions` whitelist and the exact field shapes) is in `templates/beads/default.yaml`.
295	
296	### Why YAML, not `bd formula` + `bd mol`
297	
298	The 2026-04-22 tooling survey flagged a cross-cutting finding that most bd-adjacent tools use `bd` as storage-only and ignore the declarative workflow layer (`bd formula`, `bd mol`, `bd cook`, `bd swarm`). A pre-ship probe (J121-7bv.1) tested whether `templates/beads/default.yaml` should be expressed as a bd formula instead. Verdict: **no**.
299	
300	- `bd formula` describes an **issue-DAG spawn template** — variables, steps, labels on spawned children, parent/child edges, composition via `extends`/`compose`. Cooked via `bd cook` into a proto; instantiated via `bd mol pour` into real beads.
301	- `templates/beads/default.yaml` describes **runtime coordinator behavior constraints** — `allowed_transitions` whitelist, `transition_prefix` on notes, `can_add`/`can_remove` per label namespace, metadata rewrite timing, `bd remember` format, comments disabled.
302	- Overlap is ~20% (the `create` subset — labels and parent required on spawned beads). The other ~80% has no CLI-level bd enforcement; a formula cannot constrain what the coordinator does to an *existing* bead it did not spawn.
303	
304	Converting the 20% would split the policy across two files and two mental models without retiring the YAML side. Ship YAML; the coordinator reads it at session start and self-enforces. Revisit if bd grows runtime-policy primitives (e.g., a `bd gate` shape that blocks status transitions outside an allowed set).
305	
306	## Notifications
307	
308	Two categories, driven by session-scoped hooks + direct invocation. The active `templates/alerts/<name>.yaml` declares sounds and nag cadences; dispatcher (`scripts/lib/notify-dispatcher.sh`) consults it via the message-prefix convention.
309	
310	### Category: io_block
311	
312	Fired automatically by the session's `Notification` hook when Claude Code needs user input (tool approval, idle prompt). Because hooks are session-scoped (the hook lives in `<session-dir>/.claude/settings.local.json`), the dispatcher fires unconditionally — no marker-gating.
313	
314	Characteristics:
315	- Soft sound (`IM` via BurntToast v1.1.0 `-Sound`; falls back to `Reminder` if the shim's ValidSet is narrower)
316	- Non-urgent; ephemeral on-screen; migrates to Action Center
317	- **Re-nag on 60s cadence** while blocked, via `scripts/lib/nag-repeater.sh` (detached)
318	- Hard cap: 30 re-nag iterations (30 minutes)
319	- Nag killed by `PreToolUse` hook's `scripts/lib/preuse-cancel-nag.sh` (= user responded; a tool call is about to proceed)
320	
321	### Category: decision_block
322	
323	Fired directly by the coordinator when it writes a decision-log entry with `blocking: true`:
324	
325	```sh
326	wsl-notify-send --urgent --sound=Alarm "Claude Code [$AUTOSESSION_SLUG]: Decision needed: <short title>"
327	```
328	
329	Characteristics:
330	- Urgent BurntToast (`-Urgent`) → persistent on-screen until dismissed
331	- Alarm sound
332	- Single fire, no re-nag (persistence makes re-nag redundant)
333	
334	Coordinator halts after firing and writes the pending decision ID to `state.json`. Resumes only when the user has written `Resolution:` into the decision-log entry.
335	
336	### Session scoping
337	
338	Hooks live in `<session-dir>/.claude/settings.local.json` and only load when Claude Code's cwd is the session dir. Interactive sessions never see these hooks. Concurrent auto-sessions each have their own settings file — no cross-contamination, no shared state dir.
339	
340	Hook invocations are wrapped via `scripts/lib/hook-wrapper.sh`, which writes paired `hook_started` / `hook_ended` records to `<session-dir>/hook.log` under `flock`, with a watchdog timeout (default 30s, `AUTOSESSION_HOOK_TIMEOUT` overrides). The real hook's stdout passes through untouched (preserving Claude Code's hook-response protocol); stderr is captured into the log, bounded at 1KB per record.
341	
342	## Sandbox mechanics
343	
344	Two orthogonal dimensions + a pre-empt. Each repo's resolved sandbox is a `(location, branch)` tuple, or `read_only: true` which skips the dimensions.
345	
346	### Pre-empt: `read_only`
347	
348	Coordinator may read files via `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `git log`, etc. All writes refused. If the coordinator wants to modify a read-only repo, it must halt, log as a blocking decision, and wait for user re-scope or explicit authorization.
349	
350	### Location dimension
351	
352	**`in-place`** — operate on the repo in its canonical filesystem location. Concurrent sessions or interactive shells may touch the same repo; coordinator verifies the working tree is clean before claiming a bead and refuses to proceed on unexpected state.
353	
354	**`clone`** — first action: `git clone <canonical-path> <session-dir>/clones/<repo-name>`. Coordinator operates entirely within the clone; canonical stays on its checked-out branch. At shutdown: push committed branches to canonical. Clone retained after session end for user review.
355	
356	### Branch dimension
357	
358	**`current`** — commits land on the repo's currently checked-out branch at session start. Coordinator verifies the branch matches expectations (typically `main`) before proceeding.
359	
360	**`feature`** — first action after location setup: `git checkout -b feature/auto-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>` from HEAD. All commits land on this branch. **Never** `git merge`, `git rebase`, or `git push origin main` during the session. At shutdown: `git push origin feature/auto-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>` lands the branch; never merged to main by the coordinator.
361	
362	### Pre-authorized push list
363	
364	Independent of sandbox config: the project's pre-authorized push list determines whether the coordinator may push *without* asking. For J121 this is `.beads/`, `tasks/`, and chezmoi feature branches (but **not** chezmoi main). Repos not on this list require the coordinator to halt at shutdown and ask the user to authorize the push.
365	
366	## Per-repo templates
367	
368	Templates under `templates/repos/*.md` carry frontmatter declaring applicability and optional sandbox pins:
369	
370	```yaml
371	---
372	name: chezmoi
373	applies_to:
374	  repo_paths:
375	    - ~/.local/share/chezmoi
376	  repo_name_patterns:
377	    - "*chezmoi*"
378	sandbox:
379	  location: clone                  # optional; pins location — user cannot downgrade
380	  branch: feature                  # optional; pins branch — user cannot downgrade
381	  # read_only: false               # optional; if true, the other fields are ignored
382	---
383	```
384	
385	Matches are embedded verbatim into `prompt.md` under "## Repo conventions: <repo>". Each `sandbox` field is independently optional.
386	
387	## Convention templates
388	
389	Templates under `templates/conventions/*.md` are opt-in at elicit time. Selected templates are embedded into `prompt.md` under "## Conventions". They are also consumed by the review agent (which loads them by path).
390	
391	Use for code-style preferences, commit-message patterns, project-specific patterns the review agent should check compliance against. Frontmatter `checkable_by_review: true` tells session-reviewer to enumerate rules and report complies / violates / n/a per rule.
392	
393	## Permissions templates
394	
395	Autonomous sessions benefit from a larger default allowlist than interactive sessions — every Claude Code permission prompt halts the coordinator and fires an io_block nag until the user responds. Pre-authorizing the specific commands the coordinator needs keeps the run flowing without widening the project's committed `settings.json`.
396	
397	Templates live under `templates/permissions/*.yaml`. The canonical file is `default.yaml`. Structure:
398	
399	1. **`autosession_scripts`** — the skill's own `scripts/lib/` entries.
400	   - `allowed`: empty under `default.yaml` (every lib script is hook-only).
401	   - `disallowed`: scripts the skill refuses to merge, each with a `reason`. Functions as policy AND documentation — a user who writes `Bash(~/.claude/skills/auto-session/scripts/lib/*)` sees why that's the wrong shape.
402	2. **`command_permissions`** — opt-in groups, all commented out by default. User uncomments the groups the coordinator needs (bd queries, git writes, chezmoi renders, dotnet build, Jira MCP reads, etc.). Each entry has `pattern` (merged) and `purpose` (discarded).
403	
404	### Merge behavior
405	
406	At elicit time:
407	1. Read `templates/permissions/default.yaml` (or user-chosen alternate).
408	2. Confirm `autosession_scripts.allowed` (empty by default, so often a no-op).
409	3. Show uncommented `command_permissions` groups; user toggles.
410	4. Write resolved entries to **parent project's** `<project-root>/.claude/settings.local.json` under `permissions.allow`:
411	   - Idempotent: duplicates dropped.
412	   - Surgical: `jq`-based merge preserves all other keys.
413	   - Local-only: never touches the committed `settings.json` or `~/.claude/settings.json`.
414	5. Record the merged patterns in `prompt.md` under "## Permissions granted for this session".
415	
416	### Removing permissions
417	
418	Removing an entry from the template does *not* retroactively prune live entries from `settings.local.json`. Permissions added during a session remain unless the user edits them out. A future `/uninstall-permissions` command would automate this; v1 does not include it.
419	
420	### Authoring custom templates
421	
422	Copy `default.yaml`, rename under `templates/permissions/<name>.yaml`, edit. The skill offers a template picker when more than one is present. Keep the policy reminders (read-only only, no interpreter wildcards, no `gh api *`, no `Bash(*:*)`).
423	
424	## Alerts templates
425	
426	Under `templates/alerts/*.yaml`. Declares per-category behavior: sound, urgency, nag cadence, persistence. `default.yaml` covers `io_block` (IM sound, 60s × 30 nag), `decision_block` (Alarm, urgent, persistent), `compaction` (Reminder, one-shot), `session_end` (Reminder, one-shot). Custom categories may be added.
427	
428	The dispatcher (`scripts/lib/notify-dispatcher.sh`) reads category from the Notification payload's `message` prefix or a `[category]` tag. Absent → defaults to `io_block`.
429	
430	## Beads templates
431	
432	Under `templates/beads/*.yaml`. The authoritative bead-mutation policy — `default.yaml` covers the standard case. Full field shape is in the template; the "Bead mutation policy" section above summarizes the important rules.
433	
434	When authoring a variant (e.g., `readonly.yaml` for sessions that should never mutate beads), keep the top-level `lifecycle` block shape so the coordinator and review agent can read both uniformly.

462	## Decision log format
463	
464	`<session-dir>/decisions.md` uses structured entries:
465	
466	```markdown
467	## D-<NNN>: <short title>
468	
469	- **Bead**: <id> (or session-level if cross-cutting)
470	- **Commit**: <sha> (or pending)
471	- **Logged at**: <ISO-8601 timestamp>
472	- **Blocking**: yes | no
473	
474	### Context
475	Why a decision was needed.
476	
477	### Alternatives considered
478	- **A**: <pros> / <cons>
479	- **B**: <pros> / <cons>
480	- **C**: <pros> / <cons>
481	
482	### Decision
483	What was chosen. Why.
484	
485	### Reversion plan
486	Specific steps to undo:
487	- Branches to revert
488	- Beads to reopen
489	- Files to delete or restore
490	- Side-effects to reverse (pushed branches, published docs, etc.)
491	
492	### Resolution
493	(empty while pending) — filled in by user on review: `accepted` | `reverted` | `deferred` with notes
494	```
495	
496	### Blocking threshold
497	
498	A decision is **blocking** iff:
499	- It gates more than one subsequent bead, OR
500	- It involves publishing to an external system (push to a shared branch, post to Jira, etc.), OR
501	- The coordinator genuinely can't pick between alternatives with the available context
502	
503	Blocking decisions halt the coordinator and fire a `decision_block` notification. Non-blocking decisions are logged with a reversion plan and the coordinator proceeds.
504	
505	## Logging
506	
507	Three parallel logs with distinct audit roles.
508	
509	### Human-readable: `<session-dir>/execution-log.md`
510	
511	Written by the coordinator only (never by subagents). Append-only. Prose entries:
512	
513	```markdown
514	## 2026-04-21 19:32 — J121-9kp.2.8 implementation
515	
516	Claimed bead; started timew interval. Read the existing wsl-notify-send shim (~/.local/bin/wsl-notify-send)
517	to understand the current `-Sound` ValidSet. Identified that `IM` is not currently accepted; the shim only
518	validates `Reminder` and `Alarm`. Delegated an implementation subagent (Sonnet) to extend the shim's
519	validation to accept the broader ValidSet; see agents/impl-a1b2/result.md.
520	
521	Committed to chezmoi feature branch `feature/auto-tooling-wave-1-3-20260421` (clone sandbox) at sha
522	abc1234. Did not push — per sandbox rules, push happens at shutdown.
523	```
524	
525	### Machine-readable: `<session-dir>/events.jsonl`
526	
527	Written by coordinator AND subagents — POSIX-atomic single-line appends. Every event one JSON object on one line, under 4KB. Schema:
528	
529	```json
530	{"ts": "2026-04-21T19:32:00-05:00", "agent": "main", "event": "bead_claimed", "bead": "J121-9kp.2.8"}
531	{"ts": "...", "agent": "main", "event": "timew_started", "tag": "J121-9kp.2.8"}
532	{"ts": "...", "agent": "impl-a1b2", "event": "subagent_started", "parent": "main", "bead": "J121-9kp.2.8", "model": "sonnet"}
533	{"ts": "...", "agent": "impl-a1b2", "event": "subagent_completed", "result_file": "agents/impl-a1b2/result.md"}
534	{"ts": "...", "agent": "main", "event": "commit", "repo": "chezmoi", "sha": "abc1234", "branch": "feature/auto-tooling-wave-1-3-20260421"}
535	{"ts": "...", "agent": "main", "event": "decision_logged", "id": "D-003", "blocking": false}
536	{"ts": "...", "agent": "main", "event": "bead_closed", "bead": "J121-9kp.2.8"}
537	```
538	
539	Event types (non-exhaustive):
540	- `session_started`, `session_paused`, `session_resumed`, `session_shutdown`
541	- `bead_claimed`, `bead_closed`, `bead_deferred`, `bead_blocked`
542	- `timew_started`, `timew_stopped`
543	- `subagent_started`, `subagent_completed`, `subagent_failed`
544	- `commit`, `push`, `branch_created`
545	- `decision_logged` (with `blocking` boolean)
546	- `landmark_review_fired`
547	- `notification_fired` (with `category` = io_block | decision_block | compaction | session_end)
548	- `compaction_occurred`
549	
550	### Hook audit: `<session-dir>/hook.log`
551	
552	Written only by `scripts/lib/hook-wrapper.sh`. Paired `hook_started` / `hook_ended` records per invocation, correlated via a nanosecond-scoped correlation ID. Writes are serialized under `flock` on `hook.log.lock`. Used for diagnosing hung hooks, timeouts, and hook-side errors without mixing them into the event stream. Opt-in daily rotation via `logrotate.d/claude-auto-session.conf` and `systemd/claude-auto-session-logrotate.{service,timer}` (user-level units).
553	
554	## Subagent patterns
555	
556	### Briefing scaffold
557	
558	Every subagent dispatch:
559	
560	1. Coordinator writes `agents/<type>-<short-id>/brief.md`:
561	   - Task statement
562	   - Relevant context (paths, sha, bead ID)
563	   - Expected deliverable format (summary in return message; full output to `result.md`)
564	   - Pointers to scratchpad entries the subagent should read (as paths, not inline content)
565	2. Coordinator invokes via `Agent` tool, passing brief contents as `prompt`.
566	3. Subagent writes full output to `agents/<type>-<short-id>/result.md`.
567	4. Subagent returns a one-paragraph summary in its reply message.
568	5. Coordinator reads only the summary; drills into `result.md` if detail is needed.
569	
570	### Result-to-file convention
571	
572	Subagents must write their full output to `result.md` and return only a summary. Keeps the coordinator's context small regardless of subagent verbosity.
573	
574	### Model tiers
575	
576	| Subagent role | Model | Rationale |
577	|---|---|---|
578	| Gap review | `haiku` | Read-only analysis; fast; cheap |
579	| Session-reviewer | `haiku` | Pattern matching against conventions; doesn't need deep reasoning |
580	| Logging helpers | `haiku` | Mechanical work |
581	| Implementation | `sonnet` | Default; balanced |
582	| Architecture escalation | `opus` | Only when coordinator flags genuine ambiguity it can't resolve |
583	
584	Coordinator picks tier per dispatch. Rationale logged in the subagent's `brief.md`.
585	
586	### timew + bd — single writer
587	
588	**Only the coordinator manages timew and bd mutations.** Subagents never invoke `bd-timew start/stop/switch`, `bd update --status`, `bd close`, `bd note`, or `bd remember`. This is a hard invariant — timew supports one active interval, and concurrent bd mutations can race on notes/metadata. Subagents may **read** bead state (`bd show`, `bd list`, `bd query`) but may not mutate it.
589	
590	## Pause / resume
591	
592	### Pause semantics
593	
594	True mid-flight pause of a running subagent is not possible (subagents run their tool-call loop to completion). Pause checkpoints happen at coordinator boundaries — between bead iterations, after each subagent returns.
595	
596	Pause triggers:
597	- User creates file `<session-dir>/PAUSE` → coordinator sees it at next boundary, writes state, exits cleanly
598	- User Ctrl+C / Esc → in-flight tool call is killed; whatever was last written is the resumable state
599	
600	### Resume
601	
602	User invokes `/auto-session --resume` (or "resume the paused session"). Skill:
603	1. Lists `tasks/sessions/*/state.json` entries where `status = paused` or `status = crashed`.
604	2. User picks one.
605	3. Skill emits the resume instruction (`cd <session-dir> && ./run.sh`).
606	4. Coordinator (new Claude Code session) reads `state.json`, rebuilds context by reading `execution-log.md` tail + `decisions.md` pending entries + `scratchpad/` summaries, resumes loop.
607	
608	No marker file to re-create — the session's `.claude/settings.local.json` is already in place.
609	
610	### Orphan timew intervals
611	
612	If `state.json` has `timew_open: true` but the user didn't stop the interval before resume, the coordinator stops it (as zero-duration or elapsed-gap per user preference, elicited at resume) and logs the orphan in `execution-log.md`.
613	
614	## Startup checklist (coordinator's responsibility)
615	
616	Before entering the execution loop:
617	1. Read `prompt.md` end-to-end.
618	2. Read `CLAUDE.md` (redundant with cwd auto-load but makes the single-writer and compaction rules explicit on cold start).
619	3. Read `state.json`; detect orphan timew intervals.
620	4. Verify active branches in `state.json` match actual git state (warn on drift).
621	5. Re-run `bd ready` to get current dependency-aware bead queue.
622	6. Emit `session_started` or `session_resumed` JSONL event.
623	
624	## Shutdown checklist
625	
626	1. Stop timew if open.
627	2. Commit + push all `in-place + current` repos with pending changes (pre-authorized only).
628	3. Push feature branches for `feature`-branch repos.
629	4. Write final `state.json` (`status: shutdown`).
630	5. Append shutdown entry to `execution-log.md`.
631	6. Emit `session_shutdown` JSONL event.
632	7. Write `<session-dir>/exit-summary.md` from `templates/exit-summary.md` (input artifact for `/import-session`).
633	
634	## Bundled: session-reviewer agent
635	
636	The skill bundles `agents/session-reviewer.md`. Available two ways:
637	- **Global**: `install.sh` symlinks it into `~/.claude/agents/` (or `chezmoi apply` handles the symlink for chezmoi-managed users).
638	- **Per-session**: `scripts/scaffold-autosession.sh` symlinks it into `<session-dir>/.claude/agents/` automatically. No global install needed for auto-sessions.
639	
640	Role: pattern-compliance review of chezmoi skill/command/agent/config changes on session feature branches. Invoked as `subagent_type: "session-reviewer"`. Reads convention templates from `templates/conventions/` by path (paths included in its brief).
641	
642	Deleting this skill (`/remove-skill auto-session`) breaks both symlinks; `chezmoi apply` or manual cleanup removes them.
643	
644	## References
645	
646	- `install.sh` — one-time global install (agent symlink only); TTY-guarded; idempotent.
647	- `scripts/scaffold-autosession.sh` — materializes a new session dir; called by the skill at step 4.
648	- `scripts/lib/hook-wrapper.sh` — two-record watchdog wrapper around any hook; paired start/end log records with correlation IDs.
649	- `scripts/lib/notify-dispatcher.sh` — Notification hook entry; fires toast + spawns nag-repeater.
650	- `scripts/lib/nag-repeater.sh` — detached re-nag loop (60s cadence, 30-iteration cap).

653	- `hooks/settings-fragment.json` — template fragment for per-session `.claude/settings.local.json`; wrapped via hook-wrapper.sh at scaffold time.
654	- `agents/session-reviewer.md` — bundled review agent.
